title: cyperus tweediei
intro: Cyperus tweediei, also known as Khas, is a type of grass-like plant native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve coughs. In traditional medicine, Cyperus tweediei is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various Ayurvedic and Unani remedies.
